raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "California's Gun Violence Prevention Program received $5 million in state grants to support county sheriff’s departments.",
    "The program aims to seize weapons from individuals legally prohibited from possessing them, as identified through the Department of Justice's Armed Prohibited Persons System (APPS).",
    "Reasons for inclusion in the APPS include felony convictions, domestic violence convictions, domestic violence restraining orders, and gun violence restraining orders.",
    "Prior pilot counties funded by a $3 million grant in the 2019-2020 budget have reported good progress in getting firearms out of the hands of prohibited individuals.",
    "The Ventura County Sheriff’s Office developed an advanced software tool that aids deputies in identifying and confiscating firearms from prohibited possessors.",
    "Ventura County's efforts have resulted in dozens of APPS listings being cleared in the jurisdiction."
  ]
}
```
